concerns.text: The plan cites 'UCI DROP' (https://huggingface.co/datasets/ucinlp/drop/...) as a source for 'reading comprehension numeric scores' to test t-test robustness. The DROP dataset is a Reading Comprehension benchmark consisting of text passages and questions, not a tabular numeric dataset suitable for direct t-test simulation without extensive, undefined NLP feature extraction. This dataset is inappropriate for the stated statistical method (parametric tests on numeric columns) and does not match the 'numeric sensor/transactional' profile of the other two cited datasets.

The plan cites 'UCI Shopper' (https://huggingface.co/datasets/jlh/uci-shopper/...) as a source for 'transactional numeric features'. The UCI Shopper dataset is primarily a classification dataset with categorical features (e.g., 'Month', 'VisitorType') and a binary target. It lacks the continuous numeric distributions required to meaningfully test the robustness of t-tests/ANOVA against Gaussian noise or outliers without significant, undefined preprocessing to generate synthetic numeric variables. The dataset modality does not fit the analysis plan.
